description_long: This object consists of three vertically stacked plant pots suspended by white cords, forming a hanging arrangement. The top pot is a plain light gray, the middle pot is a smooth blue, and the bottom pot is a reddish-brown terracotta style. Each pot contains a trailing vining plant with green leaves featuring distinctive holes, resembling a Monstera adansonii. The vines cascade down and around the pots, creating a lush, layered effect. The pots appear to be made of different materials, with the top and middle pots likely ceramic or plastic, and the bottom pot resembling terracotta. The overall design is decorative and space-efficient, ideal for adding greenery to vertical spaces in urban environments.
description: A trio of vertically hanging plant pots in gray, blue, and terracotta, each holding a trailing Monstera adansonii vine. The arrangement is suspended by cords and features lush, holey green leaves cascading down.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three pots clearly, with the trailing vines draping symmetrically around the arrangement.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the pots and the way the vines wrap around, with the blue pot most prominent.
description_view_2: Rear view with the vines and leaves visible from behind, showing the pots' backs and the full length of the hanging cords.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, emphasizing the terracotta pot and the way the vines cluster toward the right side.
